title: Italy's Antitrust Investigates A2A Over EV Charging Network
content:
Italy's antitrust regulator has begun an investigation into utility group A2A  over alleged violations of European Union rules in its electric vehicle charging services, it said.
In response, A2A said its units had respected the principles of transparency, fairness and free competition and were cooperating fully with the investigation by the regulator, known as the AGCM. "The group is confident that the dialogue with the AGCM will help confirm the correctness of its conduct," it added.
author: Analysts Account
